tags.notes: Part of Kisite-Mpunguti Marine National Park. Coral reef sloping from 2m to 32m. Known for dolphin encounters and reef fish. Re-audited 2026-06-10: generic marine life claims retained only where regionally confirmed.

Watamu Marine National Park, established 1968. Park depth range 5-24m. Named sites include Turtle Reef, The Canyon, Moray Reef/Canyon, Mida Wreck. Depth updated from 25m to 24m based on documented dive site depths. Re-audited 2026-06-10.

Fringing reef parallel to Diani Beach. 30+ named dive sites in area including Galu Reef, Kinondo Reef, Papa Mashilingi, MV Alpha Funguo Wreck. Re-audited 2026-06-10: whale shark claim retained (Oct-Mar season confirmed by multiple sources).

Vessel built 1965 at Hatlo Verksted SA, Ulsteinvik, Norway. Length: Wikipedia gives 75m/246ft; dive operator sources cite 77m/252ft. Displacement: 495 long tons (Wikipedia). Scuttled 27 Oct 2002 (Wikipedia) / 25 Oct 2002 (Ocean Tribe). Rests upright at 30m, shallowest at 12m. Confirmed marine life: groupers, barracuda, moray eels, Napoleon wrasse, big-eye snapper (xtremespots.com, divekenya.com). Depth conflict flagged in markdown. Re-audited 2026-06-10.

CORRECTED site_type reef->drift (strong tidal currents, drop-off to 35m, pelagic species). CORRECTED depth 15m->23m (sources confirm 23m/75ft max depth in creek). CORRECTED difficulty Intermediate->Advanced (multiple sources require drift dive experience). Mtwapa Drop Off drops from 10m to 35m with strong currents. Marine life confirmed: barracuda, black trevally, kingfish, spiny lobsters, potato grouper. Re-audited 2026-06-10.

CORRECTED site_type wall->reef: multiple sources consistently describe Nyali as a reef dive, not a wall dive. CORRECTED difficulty Intermediate->Beginner: described as 'all levels will enjoy' and beginner-friendly by multiple sources. Hard coral with anemone gardens. Confirmed marine life: anemonefish, eels, turtles, reef fish. Re-audited 2026-06-10.

NOTE: The historic Shimoni Caves are terrestrial coral limestone caves (slave trade history) — NOT the dive site. The dive site near Shimoni involves underwater cave/cavern exploration. CORRECTED max depth 28m->20m (sources report sandy bottom at ~20m with chimney exit at 10m). CORRECTED difficulty Advanced->Intermediate (sources confirm suitable for intermediate divers). Confirmed marine life: groupers, snappers, eels inside caves. Marine park protections apply. Re-audited 2026-06-10.

VALIDATION REMOVED: No independent source found confirming 'Sita Reef' as a named dive site. Not mentioned in Kisite-Mpunguti operator pages, ScubaBoard Kenya threads, or dive databases. Coordinates place it near/south of Kisite Island. Cannot validate with fewer than 3 independent sources. Re-audited 2026-06-10.

Funzi Island confirmed as diving base with fringing reef and proximity to Kisite-Mpunguti (25-min speedboat). Known for sea turtle nesting (green, hawksbill, leatherback). Diving accessed via boat from Shimoni or Msambweni. Mangrove channels also accessible. Re-audited 2026-06-10: specific dive site descriptions limited — no site-specific marine life sources beyond regional fauna. Validation retained at destination level (3 geographic/travel sources confirm site exists and diving available).

'Tewa' is confirmed Swahili name for giant grouper. Caves near mouth of Mida Creek within Watamu Marine Park. CORRECTED depth 18m->24m (sources confirm 8-24m range). CORRECTED difficulty Intermediate->Beginner (multiple sources: 'suitable for all levels of experience'). Confirmed marine life: giant groupers (up to 200kg/800lbs confirmed by multiple sources), lionfish, blue-spotted ribbontail rays. Re-audited 2026-06-10.
